question: Read: 'Machines which gather, store and analyse data and can upload it to the Internet.' What is the MAIN function of IoT machines?
choices: To replace human workers in factories
To gather, store, analyse and upload data to the internet
To provide entertainment and gaming experiences

question: Read: '________, which can monitor your heart rate, could be embedded in your body.' Choose from: alert, sensors, track, wearables.
In the passive exercises, choose the correct form: 'The first real smartphone ________ by a famous company.' (create)
In the passive exercises, complete: 'Our household appliances are made ________ only the highest quality materials.'
In the passive exercises, complete: 'A device this simple can be operated ________ a young child.'
In the USE OF ENGLISH passive exercises, complete: 'I expect the parcel ________ by four o'clock.' (DELIVERED)
In the USE OF ENGLISH exercise, complete: 'We ________ not to use our phones during the exam.' (TOLD — passive)
According to the speaking box, which phrase introduces data at the START of a trend description?
choices: Overall, the trend in … is upwards
During the period …, it is noticeable that …
There has been a slight increase in …

question: In the article 'Early Adopter or Laggard?', the word 'crashing' refers to a device that ________.
choices: suddenly stops working due to a fault

question: In the article 'Early Adopter or Laggard?', 'overheating' means ________.
Read: 'There has been a ________ growth in their number since they appeared early this century.' Which word fits for a fast, significant increase?
Read from the VR article: 'This can make it difficult to fit in with or to keep friends.' 'Keep friends' means ________.
choices: to maintain existing friendships over time
to find friends with similar interests

question: Read from the VR article: 'We can break off at any time and go back to our normal lives, whereas the people whose lives we're experiencing don't have that luxury.' 'Break off' here means ________.
choices: to break a piece of equipment
to stop or interrupt an activity
to return to a previous experience

question: Read: 'VR has also been used to give people the experience of being disabled, or being in a flood. But can these experiences really help us to walk in someone else's shoes?' The idiom 'walk in someone else's shoes' means to ________.
choices: understand another person's experience or situation

question: In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'If you encounter any problems, let me know' → 'If you ________ any problems, let me know.'
Read from the VR article comprehension: 'Why does the writer start by describing a shopping experience?' The correct answer is ________.
choices: to explain exactly why some people suffer from ASD
to demonstrate why having ASD can cause someone to get upset
to emphasise problems created by large shopping malls
to introduce the concept of virtual reality

question: Read: 'People started taking selfies to manage and curate what they wanted to tell the world about their physical attributes, their personality, their relationships and their hobbies.' What does 'manage and curate' suggest?
choices: People share everything about their lives spontaneously
People carefully control and select their online self-presentation
People rely on technology to manage their social lives
People use social media to manage their professional careers

question: Read: 'On a superficial level, their holiday photos look relaxed and natural, but in reality they've been very carefully planned.' What does the word 'superficial' mean here?
choices: relating to deep or meaningful things
relating only to the surface or outward appearance

question: Read: 'You can add filters to enhance how you look, remove any flaws or even change the shape or size of your facial features.' The word 'features' here refers to ________.
choices: special abilities of a smartphone app
parts of the face such as nose, eyes or mouth
the overall composition of a photograph

question: Read: 'People started taking selfies to present a flattering picture of themselves.' A 'flattering' picture ________.
choices: shows someone in an attractively positive way
is taken by a professional photographer
includes multiple people in the photo

question: Complete: 'I find the architecture of the city ________.' (Use the vocabulary extension word meaning 'attractive or interesting'.)
Complete: 'I ________ of people who learn Maths easily.' (Use the vocabulary extension word meaning 'wanting something someone else has'.)
Read: 'Sales of the company's main product were reported to be steady.' Which impersonal passive pattern is used?
Read: 'The Internet is thought to have contained five million terabytes of data in 2010.' Which impersonal passive pattern is used?
Complete: 'The first email is known ________ by a programmer called Ray Tomlinson.' (send)
Read: 'Unfortunately, it is not known what the message was as Tomlinson can't remember!' This uses Pattern ________.
In the for-and-against essay, a good essay should 'explain how arguments made by others are ________ as well as point out any weaknesses in them.'

Read from 'Our Personal Data': 'In conclusion, it seems obvious that while people remain largely unaware and unable to make informed choices, the ability of companies to collect data should be limited.' What writing technique is used?
choices: Summarising the topic and giving a clear recommendation

question: Combine: 'There were several cyber attacks last year' + 'most companies have not changed their security systems' using 'in spite of'. Which is correct?
choices: In spite of the several cyber attacks, most companies changed their systems
In spite of there having been several cyber attacks last year, most companies have not changed their security systems
In spite of several attacks last year although companies haven't changed systems
In spite, most companies have not changed their security systems

question: In the essay writing box, which phrase is used in the introduction to 'outline what you are going to discuss'?
choices: In conclusion, it seems obvious that …
In spite of these potential benefits …

question: According to the Life Skills section on social media, one example of using social media for education is ________.
According to the Life Skills section, social media sites can track the user's location. You should NOT leave location services on permanently because ________.
choices: it shares your private data with advertisers
you do not want everyone to know where you are all the time
it causes your phone to crash

question: According to the Life Skills section, Tip 1 'Don't post anything online without thinking about its consequences first' is matched with: ________.
choices: Remember that posting privately doesn't necessarily protect you
Add new posts to push negative content further down
Set up an alert to let you know when someone mentions you
Don't share anything that belongs to anyone else without permission

question: In the revision exercises, 'It is expected that self-driving cars ________ the number of traffic accidents.' (reduce)
In the revision exercises, 'It is claimed that virtual reality experiences ________ the same as the real thing.'
In the passive exercises, 'The watch clearly ________ before ________ from your factory.' The correct forms are ________.
choices: had not been checked / to be sent

question: In the impersonal passive exercises, complete: 'I ________ to do this in my last job.' (expect — negative)
Read from the VR article: 'While we can experience the sounds and sights of a flood, we don't come up against the same difficulties.' What does this contrast show?
choices: VR is completely identical to real experience
There is still a fundamental gap between VR and actually living through a difficult situation
VR is better than real experience for learning empathy
People who use VR feel no empathy at all

question: Read: 'It is important to have a positive digital footprint.' This advice comes from the ________.
choices: Life Skills section on managing your online image

question: Read: 'New devices often come with compatibility issues, so that you also need to buy new adaptors or cables. Being a pioneer also means dealing with any bugs or glitches that the manufacturers didn't or couldn't predict.' This passage is from ________.
choices: the Life Skills section on social media
the article Early Adopter or Laggard?
